title: trichophyllum lanceolatum
intro: Trichophyllum lanceolatum, also known as Lance-leaved Trichophyllum, is a type of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the subclass Jungermanniidae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Trichophyllum lanceolatum include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve liver function. Trichophyllum lanceolatum can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support liver health and detoxification.
